$comment: Per-brand config — lives at brand/<slug>/client-config.json (gitignored runtime data). This example documents the shape, focusing on how each cron's publish destination is configured. Created/edited by MC client creation + foundation skills; safe to edit by hand.
name: My Client
$comment_publish: Brand-level default transport for cron output. 'slack' today; the publish registry (src/lib/publish/registry.ts) can add 'discord'/'telegram' later. Each cron may override it.
$comment_channels: Optional friendly-name → channel-id map. Reference these ids from crons.<x>.publish_channel, or put the channel id directly on the cron.
$comment_crons: Per-cron config. enabled + schedule + tz drive scheduling; publish_transport (optional, defaults to publish.default_transport → slack) and publish_channel decide WHERE the cron publishes via POST /api/integrations/publish. Never hardcode a channel id in a skill/prompt — it comes from here.
